translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He who receives you receives Me, and he who receives Me receives the One who sent Me.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Whoever receives a prophet because he is a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward, and whoever receives a righteous man because he is a righteous man will receive a righteous man’s reward.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And if anyone gives even a cup of cold water to one of these little ones because he is My disciple, truly I tell you, he will never lose his reward.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: After Jesus had finished instructing His twelve disciples, He went on from there to teach and preach in their cities.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Meanwhile John heard in prison about the works of Christ, and he sent his disciples
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: to ask Him, “Are You the One who was to come, or should we look for someone else?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus replied, “Go back and report to John what you hear and see:
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The blind receive sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear, the dead are raised, and the good news is preached to the poor.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Blessed is the one who does not fall away on account of Me.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: As John’s disciples were leaving, Jesus began to speak to the crowds about John: “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed swaying in the wind?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Otherwise, what did you go out to see? A man dressed in fine clothes? Look, those who wear fine clothing are found in kings’ palaces.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: What then did you go out to see? A prophet? Yes, I tell you, and more than a prophet.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: This is the one about whom it is written: ‘Behold, I will send My messenger ahead of You, who will prepare Your way before You.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Truly I tell you, among those born of women there has risen no one greater than John the Baptist. Yet even the least in the kingdom of heaven is greater than he.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: From the days of John the Baptist until now, the kingdom of heaven has been subject to violence, and the violent lay claim to it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For all the Prophets and the Law prophesied until John.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And if you are willing to accept it, he is the Elijah who was to come.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He who has ears, let him hear.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: To what can I compare this generation? They are like children sitting in the marketplaces and calling out to others:
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: ‘We played the flute for you, and you did not dance; we sang a dirge, and you did not mourn.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon!’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Look at this glutton and drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is vindicated by her actions.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus began to denounce the cities in which most of His miracles had been performed, because they did not repent.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Tyre and Sidon, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But I tell you, it will be more bearable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And you, Capernaum, will you be lifted up to heaven? No, you will be brought down to Hades! For if the miracles that were performed in you had been performed in Sodom, it would have remained to this day.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But I tell you that it will be more bearable for Sodom on the day of judgment than for you.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: At that time Jesus declared, “I praise You, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, because You have hidden these things from the wise and learned, and revealed them to little children.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Yes, Father, for this was well-pleasing in Your sight.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: All things have been entrusted to Me by My Father. No one knows the Son except the Father, and no one knows the Father except the Son and those to whom the Son chooses to reveal Him.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Come to Me, all you who are weary and burdened, and I will give you rest.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Take My yoke upon you and learn from Me; for I am gentle and humble in heart, and you will find rest for your souls.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For My yoke is easy and My burden is light.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: At that time Jesus went through the grainfields on the Sabbath. His disciples were hungry and began to pick the heads of grain and eat them.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When the Pharisees saw this, they said to Him, “Look, Your disciples are doing what is unlawful on the Sabbath.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus replied, “Have you not read what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He entered the house of God, and he and his companions ate the consecrated bread, which was not lawful for them to eat, but only for the priests.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Or haven’t you read in the Law that on the Sabbath the priests in the temple break the Sabbath and yet are innocent?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But I tell you that something greater than the temple is here.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: If only you had known the meaning of ‘I desire mercy, not sacrifice,’ you would not have condemned the innocent.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Moving on from there, Jesus entered their synagogue,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and a man with a withered hand was there. In order to accuse Jesus, they asked Him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He replied, “If one of you has a sheep and it falls into a pit on the Sabbath, will he not take hold of it and lift it out?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: How much more valuable is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus said to the man, “Stretch out your hand.” So he stretched it out, and it was restored to full use, just like the other.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But the Pharisees went out and plotted how they might kill Jesus.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Aware of this, Jesus withdrew from that place. Large crowds followed Him, and He healed them all,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: warning them not to make Him known.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Here is My Servant, whom I have chosen, My beloved, in whom My soul delights. I will put My Spirit on Him, and He will proclaim justice to the nations.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He will not quarrel or cry out; no one will hear His voice in the streets.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: A bruised reed He will not break, and a smoldering wick He will not extinguish, till He leads justice to victory.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: In His name the nations will put their hope.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then a demon-possessed man who was blind and mute was brought to Jesus, and He healed the man so that he could speak and see.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The crowds were astounded and asked, “Could this be the Son of David?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But when the Pharisees heard this, they said, “Only by Beelzebul, the prince of demons, does this man drive out demons.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself will be laid waste, and every city or household divided against itself will not stand.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: If Satan drives out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then can his kingdom stand?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And if I drive out demons by Beelzebul, by whom do your sons drive them out? So then, they will be your judges.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But if I drive out demons by the Spirit of God, then the kingdom of God has come upon you.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Or again, how can anyone enter a strong man’s house and steal his possessions, unless he first ties up the strong man? Then he can plunder his house.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He who is not with Me is against Me, and he who does not gather with Me scatters.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man will be forgiven, but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven, either in this age or in the one to come.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Make a tree good and its fruit will be good, or make a tree bad and its fruit will be bad; for a tree is known by its fruit.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: You brood of vipers, how can you who are evil say anything good? For out of the overflow of the heart, the mouth speaks.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The good man brings good things out of his good store of treasure, and the evil man brings evil things out of his evil store of treasure.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But I tell you that men will give an account on the day of judgment for every careless word they have spoken.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For by your words you will be acquitted, and by your words you will be condemned.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then some of the scribes and Pharisees said to Him, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from You.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus replied, “A wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of the prophet Jonah.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the great fish, so the Son of Man will be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The men of Nineveh will stand at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for they repented at the preaching of Jonah, and now One greater than Jonah is here.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The Queen of the South will rise at the judgment with this generation and condemn it; for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon, and now One greater than Solomon is here.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When an unclean spirit comes out of a man, it passes through arid places seeking rest and does not find it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then it says, ‘I will return to the house I left.’ On its return, it finds the house vacant, swept clean, and put in order.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then it goes and brings with it seven other spirits more wicked than itself, and they go in and dwell there; and the final plight of that man is worse than the first. So will it be with this wicked generation.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: While Jesus was still speaking to the crowds, His mother and brothers stood outside, wanting to speak to Him.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Someone told Him, “Look, Your mother and brothers are standing outside, wanting to speak to You.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But Jesus replied, “Who is My mother, and who are My brothers?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Pointing to His disciples, He said, “Here are My mother and My brothers.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For whoever does the will of My Father in heaven is My brother and sister and mother.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: That same day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the sea.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Such large crowds gathered around Him that He got into a boat and sat down, while all the people stood on the shore.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And He told them many things in parables, saying, “A farmer went out to sow his seed.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And as he was sowing, some seed fell along the path, and the birds came and devoured it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Some fell on rocky ground, where it did not have much soil. It sprang up quickly because the soil was shallow.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But when the sun rose, the seedlings were scorched, and they withered because they had no root.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Other seed fell among thorns, which grew up and choked the seedlings.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Still other seed fell on good soil and produced a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He who has ears, let him hear.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Why do You speak to the people in parables?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He replied, “The knowledge of the mysteries of the kingdom of heaven has been given to you, but not to them.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Whoever has will be given more, and he will have an abundance. Whoever does not have, even what he has will be taken away from him.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: This is why I speak to them in parables: ‘Though seeing, they do not see; though hearing, they do not hear or understand.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled: ‘You will be ever hearing but never understanding; you will be ever seeing but never perceiving.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For this people’s heart has grown callous; they hardly hear with their ears, and they have closed their eyes. Otherwise they might see with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their hearts, and turn, and I would heal them.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But blessed are your eyes because they see, and your ears because they hear.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For truly I tell you, many prophets and righteous men longed to see what you see but did not see it, and to hear what you hear but did not hear it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Consider, then, the parable of the sower:
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When anyone hears the message of the kingdom but does not understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away what was sown in his heart. This is the seed sown along the path.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The seed sown on rocky ground is the one who hears the word and at once receives it with joy.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But since he has no root, he remains for only a season. When trouble or persecution comes because of the word, he quickly falls away.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The seed sown among the thorns is the one who hears the word, but the worries of this life and the deceitfulness of wealth choke the word, and it becomes unfruitful.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But the seed sown on good soil is the one who hears the word and understands it. He indeed bears fruit and produces a crop—a hundredfold, sixtyfold, or thirtyfold.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But while everyone was asleep, his enemy came and sowed weeds among the wheat, and slipped away.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When the wheat sprouted and bore grain, then the weeds also appeared.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The owner’s servants came to him and said, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where then did the weeds come from?’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: ‘An enemy did this,’ he replied. So the servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and pull them up?’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: ‘No,’ he said, ‘if you pull the weeds now, you might uproot the wheat with them.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Let both grow together until the harvest. At that time I will tell the harvesters: First collect the weeds and tie them in bundles to be burned; then gather the wheat into my barn.’”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He put before them another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like a mustard seed that a man planted in his field.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Although it is the smallest of all seeds, yet it grows into the largest of garden plants and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and nest in its branches.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He told them still another parable: “The kingdom of heaven is like leaven that a woman took and mixed into three measures of flour, until all of it was leavened.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus spoke all these things to the crowds in parables. He did not tell them anything without using a parable.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: So was fulfilled what was spoken through the prophet: “I will open My mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden since the foundation of the world.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus dismissed the crowds and went into the house. His disciples came to Him and said, “Explain to us the parable of the weeds in the field.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He replied, “The One who sows the good seed is the Son of Man.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The field is the world, and the good seed represents the sons of the kingdom. The weeds are the sons of the evil one,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and the enemy who sows them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the harvesters are angels.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: As the weeds are collected and burned in the fire, so will it be at the end of the age.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The Son of Man will send out His angels, and they will weed out of His kingdom every cause of sin and all who practice lawlessness.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And they will throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the kingdom of their Father. He who has ears, let him hear.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The kingdom of heaven is like treasure hidden in a field. When a man found it, he hid it again, and in his joy he went and sold all he had and bought that field.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Again, the kingdom of heaven is like a merchant in search of fine pearls.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When he found one very precious pearl, he went away and sold all he had and bought it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Once again, the kingdom of heaven is like a net that was cast into the sea and caught all kinds of fish.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When it was full, the men pulled it ashore. Then they sat down and sorted the good fish into containers, but threw the bad away.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: So will it be at the end of the age: The angels will come and separate the wicked from the righteous,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and throw them into the fiery furnace, where there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Have you understood all these things?” “Yes,” they answered.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then He told them, “For this reason, every scribe who has been discipled in the kingdom of heaven is like a homeowner who brings out of his storeroom new treasures as well as old.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When Jesus had finished these parables, He withdrew from that place.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Coming to His hometown, He taught the people in their synagogue, and they were astonished. “Where did this man get such wisdom and miraculous powers?” they asked.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Isn’t this the carpenter’s son? Isn’t His mother’s name Mary, and aren’t His brothers James, Joseph, Simon, and Judas?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Aren’t all His sisters with us as well? Where then did this man get all these things?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And they took offense at Him. But Jesus said to them, “Only in his hometown and in his own household is a prophet without honor.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And He did not do many miracles there, because of their unbelief.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: At that time Herod the tetrarch heard the reports about Jesus
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptist; he has risen from the dead! That is why miraculous powers are at work in him.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Now Herod had arrested John and bound him and put him in prison on account of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: because John had been telling him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Although Herod wanted to kill John, he was afraid of the people, because they regarded John as a prophet.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: On Herod’s birthday, however, the daughter of Herodias danced before them and pleased Herod
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: so much that he promised with an oath to give to her whatever she asked.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Prompted by her mother, she said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptist.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The king was grieved, but because of his oaths and his guests, he ordered that her wish be granted
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and sent to have John beheaded in the prison.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: John’s head was brought in on a platter and presented to the girl, who carried it to her mother.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then John’s disciples came and took his body and buried it. And they went and informed Jesus.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When Jesus heard about John, He withdrew by boat privately to a solitary place. But the crowds found out about it and followed Him on foot from the towns.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When He stepped ashore and saw a large crowd, He had compassion on them and healed their sick.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When evening came, the disciples came to Him and said, “This is a desolate place, and the hour is already late. Dismiss the crowds so they can go to the villages and buy themselves some food.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “They do not need to go away,” Jesus replied. “You give them something to eat.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “We have here only five loaves of bread and two fish,” they answered.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Bring them here to Me,” Jesus said.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And He directed the crowds to sit down on the grass. Taking the five loaves and the two fish and looking up to heaven, He spoke a blessing. Then He broke the loaves and gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up twelve basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: About five thousand men were fed, in addition to women and children.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go on ahead of Him to the other side, while He dismissed the crowds.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: After He had sent them away, He went up on the mountain by Himself to pray. When evening came, He was there alone,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: but the boat was already far from land, buffeted by the waves because the wind was against it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: During the fourth watch of the night, Jesus went out to them, walking on the sea.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When the disciples saw Him walking on the sea, they were terrified. “It’s a ghost!” they said, and cried out in fear.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But Jesus spoke up at once: “Take courage! It is I. Do not be afraid.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Lord, if it is You,” Peter replied, “command me to come to You on the water.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Come,” said Jesus. Then Peter got down out of the boat, walked on the water, and came toward Jesus.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But when he saw the strength of the wind, he was afraid, and beginning to sink, cried out, “Lord, save me!”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Immediately Jesus reached out His hand and took hold of Peter. “You of little faith,” He said, “why did you doubt?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And when they had climbed back into the boat, the wind died down.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then those who were in the boat worshiped Him, saying, “Truly You are the Son of God!”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When they had crossed over, they landed at Gennesaret.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And when the men of that place recognized Jesus, they sent word to all the surrounding region. People brought all the sick to Him
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and begged Him just to let them touch the fringe of His cloak. And all who touched Him were healed.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then some Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem and asked,
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Why do Your disciples break the tradition of the elders? They do not wash their hands before they eat.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus replied, “And why do you break the command of God for the sake of your tradition?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For God said, ‘Honor your father and mother’ and ‘Anyone who curses his father or mother must be put to death.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But you say that if anyone says to his father or mother, ‘Whatever you would have received from me is a gift devoted to God,’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: he need not honor his father or mother with it. Thus you nullify the word of God for the sake of your tradition.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: You hypocrites! Isaiah prophesied correctly about you:
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: ‘These people honor Me with their lips, but their hearts are far from Me.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: They worship Me in vain; they teach as doctrine the precepts of men.’”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus called the crowd to Him and said, “Listen and understand.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: A man is not defiled by what enters his mouth, but by what comes out of it.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the disciples came to Him and said, “Are You aware that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But Jesus replied, “Every plant that My heavenly Father has not planted will be pulled up by its roots.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Disregard them! They are blind guides. If a blind man leads a blind man, both will fall into a pit.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Peter said to Him, “Explain this parable to us.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Do you still not understand?” Jesus asked.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Do you not yet realize that whatever enters the mouth goes into the stomach and then is eliminated?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But the things that come out of the mouth come from the heart, and these things defile a man.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murder, adultery, sexual immorality, theft, false testimony, and slander.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: These are what defile a man, but eating with unwashed hands does not defile him.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Leaving that place, Jesus withdrew to the district of Tyre and Sidon.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And a Canaanite woman from that region came to Him, crying out, “Lord, Son of David, have mercy on me! My daughter is miserably possessed by a demon.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But Jesus did not answer a word. So His disciples came and urged Him, “Send her away, for she keeps crying out after us.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: He answered, “I was sent only to the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The woman came and knelt before Him. “Lord, help me!” she said.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But Jesus replied, “It is not right to take the children’s bread and toss it to the dogs.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Yes, Lord,” she said, “even the dogs eat the crumbs that fall from their master’s table.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “O woman,” Jesus answered, “your faith is great! Let it be done for you as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that very hour.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Moving on from there, Jesus went along the Sea of Galilee. Then He went up on a mountain and sat down.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Large crowds came to Him, bringing the lame, the blind, the crippled, the mute, and many others, and laid them at His feet, and He healed them.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The crowd was amazed when they saw the mute speaking, the crippled restored, the lame walking, and the blind seeing. And they glorified the God of Israel.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus called His disciples to Him and said, “I have compassion for this crowd, because they have already been with Me three days and have nothing to eat. I do not want to send them away hungry, or they may faint along the way.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The disciples replied, “Where in this desolate place could we find enough bread to feed such a large crowd?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “How many loaves do you have?” Jesus asked. “Seven,” they replied, “and a few small fish.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And He instructed the crowd to sit down on the ground.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Taking the seven loaves and the fish, He gave thanks and broke them. Then He gave them to the disciples, and the disciples gave them to the people.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: They all ate and were satisfied, and the disciples picked up seven basketfuls of broken pieces that were left over.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: A total of four thousand men were fed, in addition to women and children.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: After Jesus had dismissed the crowds, He got into the boat and went to the region of Magadan.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the Pharisees and Sadducees came and tested Jesus by asking Him to show them a sign from heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But He replied, “When evening comes, you say, ‘The weather will be fair, for the sky is red,’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and in the morning, ‘Today it will be stormy, for the sky is red and overcast.’ You know how to interpret the appearance of the sky, but not the signs of the times.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: A wicked and adulterous generation demands a sign, but none will be given it except the sign of Jonah.” Then He left them and went away.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When they crossed to the other side, the disciples forgot to take bread.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Watch out!” Jesus told them. “Beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: They discussed this among themselves and concluded, “It is because we did not bring any bread.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Aware of their conversation, Jesus said, “You of little faith, why are you debating among yourselves about having no bread?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Do you still not understand? Do you not remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many basketfuls you gathered?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many basketfuls you gathered?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: How do you not understand that I was not telling you about bread? But beware of the leaven of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then they understood that He was not telling them to beware of the leaven used in bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When Jesus came to the region of Caesarea Philippi, He questioned His disciples: “Who do people say the Son of Man is?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: They replied, “Some say John the Baptist; others say Elijah; and still others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “But what about you?” Jesus asked. “Who do you say I am?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus replied, “Blessed are you, Simon son of Jonah! For this was not revealed to you by flesh and blood, but by My Father in heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And I tell you that you are Peter, and on this rock I will build My church, and the gates of Hades will not prevail against it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: I will give you the keys of the kingdom of heaven. Whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then He admonished the disciples not to tell anyone that He was the Christ.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: From that time on Jesus began to show His disciples that He must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things at the hands of the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and that He must be killed and on the third day be raised to life.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Peter took Him aside and began to rebuke Him. “Far be it from You, Lord!” he said. “This shall never happen to You!”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But Jesus turned and said to Peter, “Get behind Me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to Me. For you do not have in mind the things of God, but the things of men.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus told His disciples, “If anyone wants to come after Me, he must deny himself and take up his cross and follow Me.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For whoever wants to save his life will lose it, but whoever loses his life for My sake will find it.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: What will it profit a man if he gains the whole world, yet forfeits his soul? Or what can a man give in exchange for his soul?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For the Son of Man will come in His Father’s glory with His angels, and then He will repay each one according to what he has done.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Truly I tell you, some who are standing here will not taste death before they see the Son of Man coming in His kingdom.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: After six days Jesus took with Him Peter, James, and John the brother of James, and led them up a high mountain by themselves.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: There He was transfigured before them. His face shone like the sun, and His clothes became as white as the light.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Suddenly Moses and Elijah appeared before them, talking with Jesus.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Peter said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If You wish, I will put up three shelters—one for You, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: While Peter was still speaking, a bright cloud enveloped them, and a voice from the cloud said, “This is My beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to Him!”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When the disciples heard this, they fell facedown in terror.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus came over and touched them. “Get up,” He said. “Do not be afraid.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And when they looked up, they saw no one except Jesus.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: As they were coming down the mountain, Jesus commanded them, “Do not tell anyone about this vision until the Son of Man has been raised from the dead.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: The disciples asked Him, “Why then do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus replied, “Elijah does indeed come, and he will restore all things.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But I tell you that Elijah has already come, and they did not recognize him, but have done to him whatever they wished. In the same way, the Son of Man will suffer at their hands.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the disciples understood that He was speaking to them about John the Baptist.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When they came to the crowd, a man came up to Jesus and knelt before Him.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Lord, have mercy on my son,” he said. “He has seizures and is suffering terribly. He often falls into the fire or into the water.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: I brought him to Your disciples, but they could not heal him.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “O unbelieving and perverse generation!” Jesus replied. “How long must I remain with you? How long must I put up with you? Bring the boy here to Me.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Jesus rebuked the demon, and it came out of the boy, and he was healed from that moment.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Afterward the disciples came to Jesus privately and asked, “Why couldn’t we drive it out?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Because you have so little faith,” He answered. “For truly I tell you, if you have faith the size of a mustard seed, you can say to this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move. Nothing will be impossible for you.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When they gathered together in Galilee, Jesus told them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered into the hands of men.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: They will kill Him, and on the third day He will be raised to life.” And the disciples were deeply grieved.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: After they had arrived in Capernaum, the collectors of the two-drachma tax came to Peter and asked, “Does your Teacher pay the two drachmas?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Yes,” he answered. When Peter entered the house, Jesus preempted him. “What do you think, Simon?” He asked. “From whom do the kings of the earth collect customs and taxes: from their own sons, or from others?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “From others,” Peter answered. “Then the sons are exempt,” Jesus declared.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “But so that we may not offend them, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take the first fish you catch. When you open its mouth, you will find a four-drachma coin. Take it and give it to them for My tax and yours.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: At that time the disciples came to Jesus and asked, “Who then is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus invited a little child to stand among them.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: “Truly I tell you,” He said, “unless you change and become like little children, you will never enter the kingdom of heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Therefore, whoever humbles himself like this little child is the greatest in the kingdom of heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And whoever welcomes a little child like this in My name welcomes Me.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But if anyone causes one of these little ones who believe in Me to stumble, it would be better for him to have a large millstone hung around his neck and to be drowned in the depths of the sea.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Woe to the world for the causes of sin. These stumbling blocks must come, but woe to the man through whom they come!
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: If your hand or your foot causes you to sin, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life crippled or lame than to have two hands and two feet and be thrown into the eternal fire.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And if your eye causes you to sin, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to enter life with one eye than to have two eyes and be thrown into the fire of hell.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: See that you do not look down on any of these little ones. For I tell you that their angels in heaven always see the face of My Father in heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: What do you think? If a man has a hundred sheep and one of them goes astray, will he not leave the ninety-nine on the hills and go out to search for the one that is lost?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: And if he finds it, truly I tell you, he rejoices more over that one sheep than over the ninety-nine that did not go astray.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: In the same way, your Father in heaven is not willing that any of these little ones should perish.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: If your brother sins against you, go and confront him privately. If he listens to you, you have won your brother over.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But if he will not listen, take one or two others along, so that ‘every matter may be established by the testimony of two or three witnesses.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the church. And if he refuses to listen even to the church, regard him as you would a pagan or a tax collector.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Truly I tell you, whatever you bind on earth will be bound in heaven, and whatever you loose on earth will be loosed in heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Again, I tell you truly that if two of you on the earth agree about anything you ask for, it will be done for you by My Father in heaven.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: For where two or three gather together in My name, there am I with them.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then Peter came to Jesus and asked, “Lord, how many times shall I forgive my brother who sins against me? Up to seven times?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, “I tell you, not just seven times, but seventy-seven times!
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Because of this, the kingdom of heaven is like a king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: As he began the settlements, a debtor was brought to him owing ten thousand talents.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Since the man was unable to pay, the master ordered that he be sold to pay his debt, along with his wife and children and everything he owned.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the servant fell on his knees before him. ‘Have patience with me,’ he begged, ‘and I will pay back everything.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: His master had compassion on him, forgave his debt, and released him.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But when that servant went out, he found one of his fellow servants who owed him a hundred denarii. He grabbed him and began to choke him, saying, ‘Pay back what you owe me!’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: So his fellow servant fell down and begged him, ‘Have patience with me, and I will pay you back.’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: But he refused. Instead, he went and had the man thrown into prison until he could pay his debt.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When his fellow servants saw what had happened, they were greatly distressed, and they went and recounted all of this to their master.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then the master summoned him and declared, ‘You wicked servant! I forgave all your debt because you begged me.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Shouldn’t you have had mercy on your fellow servant, just as I had on you?’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: In anger his master turned him over to the jailers to be tortured, until he should repay all that he owed.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: That is how My heavenly Father will treat each of you unless you forgive your brother from your heart.”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: When Jesus had finished saying these things, He left Galilee and went into the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Large crowds followed Him, and He healed them there.
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Then some Pharisees came and tested Him by asking, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?”
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: Jesus answered, “Have you not read that from the beginning the Creator ‘made them male and female,’
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: and said, ‘For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh’?
translations.license: Free for any use (Berean Standard Bible 2022) — bereanbible.com

translations.name: Berean Standard Bible
translations.text: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate.”
